title: Worldwide delineation of multi-tier city–regions
methods: minimum cumulative weighted distance catchment delineation

data_scope.spatial_coverage: global (213 countries/territories, all land at 30 arc-second ~1 km resolution)

core_conclusion: The first systematic worldwide delineation of city–regions classifies 30,079 urban centers across 213 countries/territories into four tiers and maps their travel-time catchments, identifying 1,403 primary city–regions at a 3 h cutoff and 4,210 at 1 h. 3.2 billion people can reach multiple urban tiers within 1 h (4.7 billion within 3 h); among people closest to towns or small cities, twice as many have easier access to intermediate than to large cities, highlighting intermediate cities' connective role and challenging primate-city-centric policy.
why_it_matters: Replaces case studies with a global, exhaustive system view; lets each location reference up to four overlapping urban tiers instead of a single center; elevates intermediate and small cities as policy targets; and ships an open Zenodo dataset/toolbox usable for regional planning, food systems, health/education access and resource management.
why_nature: Universal, transferable planet-scale framework operationalizing central place theory; couples accessibility to development and equity in the under-studied Global South; strongly policy-translatable across diverse country contexts; and an open dataset/code infrastructure contribution.
added_value_to_my_research: Nested town/small/intermediate/large catchment hierarchy seeds multi-tier R2SFCA for primary and specialized care worldwide
Global patch dataset + assumed tier hierarchy can be validated and reweighted with mobile-phone/GPS visitation flows
Static cost grid turned into Monte Carlo transport-investment scenario engine for equity-optimized edge additions
City–region overlaps as a global graph for network community detection and explainable-ML (MGWR/SHAP) of connectivity drivers
Patch-level tier access as a controlled frame for telehealth substitution and access-vs-outcome equity modeling
